case.title: Organized Retail Theft — Target, Fontana
case.summary: Coordinated theft ring targeting high-value electronics and fragrances across multiple visits. Suspects use booster bags and have been identified at three separate Target locations in the Inland Empire over a two-week period. Estimated combined loss exceeds $14,000.
case.narrative:
On 03/15/2026 at approximately 1420 hours, Target LP associate Derek Simmons observed two female subjects enter Store #1287. Subject 1 (later identified as Maria Delgado) proceeded directly to the electronics department and began concealing Apple AirPods and Samsung earbuds inside a foil-lined shopping bag. Subject 2 (unidentified, Hispanic female, approximately 30 years old) acted as a lookout near the department entrance.

Simmons reviewed prior CCTV footage and confirmed the same subjects had visited on 03/08 and 03/11, executing similar theft patterns. On both prior occasions, the subjects exited through the garden center emergency exit.

Fontana PD was contacted and responded. Officer Martinez (Badge #4412) arrived at 1445 hours and took the report. Subjects had already fled in a silver Honda Civic, partial plate 8ABC. LP team recovered a dropped receipt from a nearby Walmart that may link to the suspects.

Network scan through TRACE identified two potential matches at Target stores in Ontario and Rancho Cucamonga with similar suspect descriptions and MO.

suspects.name: Maria Elena Delgado
suspects.clothing: Black hoodie, blue jeans, white Nike shoes. Carrying large black tote bag (booster bag).
suspects.other_desc: Tattoo of rose on left wrist. Small scar above right eyebrow.
suspects.notes: Previously identified in ORC ring operating out of San Bernardino County. Known to fence stolen goods through social media marketplace accounts.

suspects.name: Unknown Female
suspects.clothing: Red jacket, black leggings, gray sneakers. Carrying small crossbody purse.
suspects.other_desc: Observed using a cell phone to communicate with Subject 1 during all three incidents. Possible coordinator.
suspects.notes: Acts as lookout. Positions herself near exits and uses phone—possibly texting Subject 1 about employee movements.

vehicles.description: 4-door sedan, tinted rear windows, aftermarket rims. Damage to rear bumper (right side).
vehicles.notes: Partial plate obtained from parking lot camera. Vehicle also observed at Ontario Target on 03/11.

notes.note: Contacted Ontario PD — they have a similar ORC case (ONT-2026-3388) with matching suspect description. Waiting on their CCTV footage.

TRACE network match confirmed: Rancho Cucamonga Target reported $3,200 loss on 03/12 with same MO (booster bags, electronics/fragrances, garden center exit). Their LP has plate image—running through ALPR.
Walmart receipt recovered at scene traced to a purchase made with a prepaid Visa card. Working with Walmart LP to pull transaction footage from their Fontana store on 03/14.
Fontana PD detective assigned: Det. Rachel Torres, (909) 555-0177, rtorres@fontanapd.gov. Case elevated to multi-jurisdictional investigation.

evidence.description: CCTV still — Subject 1 (Delgado) in electronics dept concealing merchandise, 03/15/2026 14:22
CCTV still — Subject 2 (unknown) acting as lookout near electronics entrance, 03/15/2026 14:24
Parking lot camera — Silver Honda Civic, partial plate 8ABC, garden center lot, 03/15/2026 14:38
Walmart receipt found at scene — prepaid Visa transaction, Fontana store, 03/14/2026
Fontana PD Incident Report #FPD-2026-11847, Officer Martinez Badge #4412
